creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
(In reply to Jason W. Bacon from comment #0)
> The Porter's Handbook (and my mentors) state that DISTVERSION should be used
> instead of PORTVERSION whenever possible (section 5.2.2):
> 
> https://www.freebsd.org/doc/en_US.ISO8859-1/books/porters-handbook/makefile-
> naming.html
> 
> The "port create" command currently inserts PORTVERSION into new skeletal
> ports, though.
> 
> Should just be a matter of updating tmpl/*.

Not (necessarily) complaining here. But; when did $DISTVERSION
become the defacto?
When I became a Maintainer some 4½ yrs ago, $PORTVERSION was
"it".

Thanks

--Chris
